Explanation: `RefreshQueue` deduplicates by tenant so same-tenant refreshes coalesce, but different tenants can still run in parallel. The rejected global-mutex alternative would unnecessarily block unrelated tenants.
</task_result>
state.title: Find queue rationale

parts.text: {"answer":"src/auth/queue.ts deduplicates refresh work per tenant so concurrent refreshes for the same tenant coalesce while unrelated tenants can still refresh in parallel; a single global mutex was rejected because it would serialize and block independent tenants unnecessarily.","evidence":{"session_id":"auth_refresh_session","blob_id":"auth_refresh_session.md","message_id":"auth_fact_3"},"rationale":"The evidence states both the intended per-tenant coalescing behavior and the rejected global-mutex alternative, which matters because the reason is avoiding unnecessary cross-tenant blocking while still deduplicating same-tenant work.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["Billing retry queue rationale","Markdown parser onboarding cleanup"]}
